MTC
METROPOLITAN
TRANSPORTATION
COMMISSION

June 7, 1990

Alameda County
ALES GIULIANI
EDWARD R. CAMPBELL

Contra Costa County
ROBERT I. SCHNEIDER
STEVE WEIR
Vice-Chair

Marin County
ROBERT B. STOCKWELL

Napa County
FRED NEGRI

San Francisco-
City and County
DOUG W. KAHN
HARRY G. BRITT

San Mateo County
JANE BAKER
TOM NOLAN

Santa Clara County
JAMES T. BEALL JR.
ROD DIRIDON
Chairperson

Solano County
JAMES SPERING

Sonoma County
WILLIAM R. LUCIUS

Representative of
Bay Area Governments
DIANNE McKENNA

S.F. Bay Conservation
and Development
Commission
ANGELO J. SIRACUSA

State Business,
Transportation and
Housing Agency
BURCH BACHTOLD

Executive Director
LAWRENCE D. DAHMS

Deputy Executive Director
WILLIAM F. HEIN

Margaret Ellison, Esq.
Senior Staff Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Newsletters (2 Cal.Code Regs. Section 18901)

Dear Ms. Ellison:

In followup to our previous telephone conversations discussing my general
questions on mass mailings and the revised FPPC regulations on mass
mailings, I write specifically with two questions concerning newsletters
and meeting announcements.

The Metropolitan Transportation Commission (MTC) is a regional
transportation planning agency created by the Legislature at Government
Code § 66500 et seq. MTC is governed by a Commission consisting of
appointed officials who, in many instances, are also elected officials.

The new FPPC regulations, at 2 Cal.Code.Regs. § 18901, permit:

(9)(A) An announcement of any meeting or event of the type listed in
paragraph (i) or (ii).

(i) An announcement sent to an elected officer's constituents
concerning a public meeting which is directly related to the
elected officer's incumbent governmental duties, which is to be
held by the elected officer, and which the elected officer
intends to attend.

(ii) An announcement of any official agency event or events
for which the agency is providing the use of its facilities or
staff or other financial support.

9(B) Any announcement provided for in under this subdivision (9)
shall not include the elected officer's photograph or signature and
may included only a single mention of the elected officer's name
except as permitted elsewhere in this regulation.

1. MTC publishes a monthly newsletter entitled TransActions, with a
mailing list consisting of approximately 7,000 copies per month.
TransActions currently includes references to MTC Commissioners only on a
commission roster block. No mention of Commissioners is referenced in

JOSEPH P. BORT METROCENTER • 101 EIGHTH STREET • OAKLAND, CA 94607-4700 • 415/464-7700 • FAX 415/464-7848

Margaret Ellison
June 7, 1990
Page two

any of the articles in TransActions. Attached for your information are copies
of the published April 1989, May 1989 and March 1990 issues of TransActions.

In light of the provisions in the regulations permitting announcements, may MTC
include announcements of meetings to be chaired or attended by an MTC
Commissioner in TransActions? For the purpose of graphically illustrating
this question, attached are mock versions of the April 1989, May 1989 and
March 1990 TransActions, with "Announcements" columns that list examples of
public meetings and announcements of official agency events, the type of which
presumably would be permissible if announced separately.

2. MTC also distributes a monthly announcement notice to other public
officials and interested citizens that lists the meeting schedules of MTC
sponsored meetings. Approximately 800 copies are mailed out per month, with
no mention of Commissioners in the notice. May we now amend such
announcements to list MTC Commissioners who plan to attend the meetings?
Attached is an example of the monthly announcement notice dated April 18,
1990, as well as a mock version of the same notice with references to MTC
Commissioners who plan to chair or participate in the meetings.

Please advise if you need additional information.

Very truly yours,

Francis Chin
General Counsel

FC:lt:9401L50
Attachments
